sections.heading: TL;DR (Too Long; Didn't Read)
content: This summary gives you the key points of our Privacy Policy in plain language. It does not replace the full policy, which you should read for complete details about how your data is handled.
<ul class="list-disc list-outside mb-4 space-y-1 pl-5">
• No accounts, no analytics, no ads. We do not operate user accounts or track how you use the App.
• On your device: Browsing, search, saved trees, and preferences work on your device. The App works offline for core features.

• Tree data: Public-realm tree information from the London Datastore (London Public Realm Trees) is included in the App under the Open Government Licence v3.0. We do not collect personal data when you view the map or Insights.

• Data rights split: Underlying source data remains licensed by original providers. The App dataset may include our team’s validation, corrections, restructuring, and user-feedback updates. Reuse of our derived in-app dataset and app-specific enhancements requires our prior written permission unless otherwise permitted by law or applicable source licence terms.

• Optional reports only: If you choose to flag a map issue, you may submit a short user-generated report. Reports are not linked to your identity (no sign-in). You can send a report via email or store it in a shared iCloud (CloudKit) database we use to receive community corrections.
• No selling or sharing of personal data with advertisers or data brokers.

sections.heading: Introduction
content: Welcome to London Tree Map. This Privacy Policy explains how we handle information when you use our application (hereafter referred to as "the App").

The App helps you explore London's public-realm trees on an interactive map: discover and filter trees, save favourites, view Insights, share a tree, or open its location in your maps app. Tree inventory information is sourced from the London Public Realm Trees dataset on the London Datastore, licensed under the Open Government Licence v3.0. Most features run on your device and offline. We are committed to data minimisation and transparency.
By using the App, you agree to the terms outlined in this Privacy Policy.

sections.heading: Collection of Personal Data
content: We distinguish between personal data (information that can identify you) and user-generated reports (optional corrections you choose to submit about map data).
What we do not collect
The App does not include:
<ul class="list-disc list-outside mb-4 space-y-1 pl-5">
• User accounts or sign-in
• Analytics, advertising identifiers, or behavioural tracking
• A backend that profiles you or logs your browsing history
We do not ask for your name, email address, or other contact details within the App.
Data processed on your device
The following stays on your device (and, where applicable, in storage mechanisms you control, such as UserDefaults or iCloud for app data on your Apple ID):
<ul class="list-disc list-outside mb-4 space-y-1 pl-5">
• Preferences and settings (for example, filters or display choices)
• Saved trees and similar local bookmarks
• Offline use of map and tree information already available in the App

Open data in the App: The bundled tree dataset describes public-realm trees (locations, species, and related attributes). It is not personal data about you. Under the OGL, the licence does not cover personal data contained in the Information; the App is not designed to extract or display personal data from that dataset.
We do not receive this on-device data unless you separately choose to submit a report (see below).
User-generated reports (optional)
If something on the map does not match what you see on the street, you may optionally submit a tree data issue report. A report is user-generated content and may include, for example:
<ul class="list-disc list-outside mb-4 space-y-1 pl-5">
• Which tree or location you are referring to
• What you believe is wrong, missing, or outdated
• Any short notes you choose to add
Reports are not linked to your identity. We do not assign reports to an account or persistent user identifier.
You can submit a report in one of two ways:

1. Email: The App may open your email client with a pre-filled message to us. What you send is up to you; we receive only what you choose to include in that email. Your email address is handled by your mail provider and is not collected or stored by us through the App itself.

2. Shared iCloud (CloudKit): The App may save the report to a shared CloudKit database operated for the purpose of collecting community corrections. Reports stored this way remain anonymous from our perspective (no account linkage). We can read these submissions to review and improve map accuracy.
Submitting a report is entirely optional. You can use the App without ever sending one.

sections.heading: Use of Personal Data
content: We use information as follows:
<ul class="list-disc list-outside mb-4 space-y-1 pl-5">
• On-device features: Tree browsing, Discover, Insights, saved trees, sharing, and offline use require no personal data collection by us.

• Reports you submit: We use user-generated reports only to investigate map issues, correct or improve tree data where appropriate, and maintain the usefulness of the App for everyone. We do not use reports for advertising or profiling.
We do not use personal data for marketing unless you have separately contacted us and agreed to further communication outside the App.

sections.heading: Sharing of Personal Data
content: We do not sell your personal data. We do not share personal data with advertisers or data brokers.

User-generated reports you submit by email are received by us directly. Reports you submit via the shared CloudKit database are accessible to us as the App maintainer for the purposes described above. We do not publish your reports as attributable to you.
We may disclose information only where required by law or to protect our rights, users, or the integrity of the service.

sections.heading: Data Provenance and Licensing
content: Where the App incorporates third-party data, that source material is licensed under the terms specified by the relevant provider (including the OGL where applicable).

We may process, standardise, validate, correct, restructure, and enrich that material to create a derived in-app dataset, including updates made by our team and follow-up changes informed by user feedback.
Rights in underlying source data remain with the original licensors. Rights in our derived dataset, compilations, enrichment, and app-specific presentation are claimed by us.

You may use underlying source data in line with its original licence terms. Reuse of our derived in-app dataset, compilations, and app-specific enhancements requires our prior written permission unless otherwise permitted by law or applicable source licence terms.

sections.heading: Third-Party Services
content: <ul class="list-disc list-outside mb-4 space-y-1 pl-5">

• London Datastore (open data): Tree inventory data is sourced from the London Public Realm Trees dataset, published via the London Datastore and licensed under the Open Government Licence v3.0. That data is included or bundled in the App for offline use; browsing the map does not send your activity to the Greater London Authority or other data publishers. Attribution: Contains public sector information licensed under the Open Government Licence v3.0. For full licensing terms, see the OGL v3.0 and our Terms of Use (Data Sources and Attribution).
• Apple (iOS, CloudKit, iCloud): If you use CloudKit submission or device storage tied to your Apple ID, Apple processes data under its own terms and privacy policies. See Apple's Privacy Policy.

• Apple Maps / sharing: If you open a tree location in your maps app or use the system share sheet, that interaction is between you and Apple (or the app you choose). We do not receive data from those actions.
• Email: If you send a report by email, your email provider handles transmission. We receive only the content you send.
The App does not integrate analytics SDKs, advertising networks, or social login providers.

sections.heading: Cookies and Other Technologies
content: London Tree Map is a native iOS application and does not use cookies or web trackers. Local storage on your device is used only to support App functionality and preferences.

sections.heading: International Data Transfers
content: We do not operate a user-account database. Processing of on-device data occurs on your device.
If you submit a report via CloudKit, Apple may process and store that data in data centres outside your country of residence, in accordance with Apple's policies and safeguards.
If you email us, your message is handled according to how you and your email provider send and store mail.

sections.heading: Data Retention & Storage
content: <ul class="list-disc list-outside mb-4 space-y-1 pl-5">
• On-device data: Saved trees, settings, and offline content remain on your device until you delete them or remove the App.
• User-generated reports (CloudKit): We retain reports for as long as needed to review map issues and maintain data quality, then delete or anonymise them when no longer required.
• User-generated reports (email): We retain email submissions only as long as needed for the same purposes, unless we must keep them longer for legal or operational reasons.

• Deletion: Deleting the App removes its local data from your device. CloudKit records you submitted are not tied to your identity; contact us if you need help regarding a specific submission sent by email.

sections.heading: Protection of Personal Data
content: We apply reasonable technical and organisational measures appropriate to the App's minimal collection model, including:
<ul class="list-disc list-outside mb-4 space-y-1 pl-5">
• No account system and no routine collection of identifiers
• Anonymous-by-design report submissions via CloudKit
• Restricted access to report data used for map maintenance
No method of transmission or storage is completely secure, but we aim to minimise risk through data minimisation and limited retention.

sections.heading: Your Privacy Rights & Choices
content: Depending on where you live (for example, the UK or EEA), you may have rights to access, rectify, erase, restrict, or object to processing, and data portability, in relation to personal data we hold about you.

Because the App does not use accounts, most data stays on your device under your control. For email reports, you can ask us about messages you have sent us. For CloudKit reports, we do not hold data linked to your identity; if you believe you submitted something in error, contact us with enough detail for us to locate it (without requiring an account).
You can stop submitting reports at any time by simply not using the report feature.
To exercise your rights or ask questions, contact us using the details in Contact Us.

sections.heading: Children & Privacy
content: The App is intended for a general audience and is not directed at children. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children.

Optional reports may contain free-text descriptions. We encourage parents or guardians to supervise younger users if they use the report feature. If you believe a child has sent us personal information by email, contact us and we will take reasonable steps to delete it.

sections.heading: Changes to This Policy
content: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in the App, legal requirements, or our practices. When we make significant changes, we will update the "Last Updated" date at the top and, where appropriate, provide notice within the App.
Continued use of the App after changes take effect constitutes your acceptance of the updated policy.

sections.heading: Contact Us
content: If you have questions about this policy or your data, please contact:
Email: hello@cumuluscoding.com
When emailing us, please include the app name first, followed by your subject in the email's subject line.
For example: London Tree Map - [Your Subject Here]
